headline: Harness engineering upgrade: deterministic slash-command contracts + preflight/postflight wrappers
highlights: Harness wrappers: added one-command preflight/postflight with structured JSONL logs (bootstrap + execution).
Public slash-command contract: README, USAGE_GUIDE, registry, and workflows are enforced together by validate_command_surface.py.
Dynamic context execution briefs: build_execution_brief supports changed-files + failing-evidence signals to reduce context drift.
/design and /marcus_init moved to script-backed command contracts with deterministic readiness/output gates.
/refactor-planning hardened: brownfield readiness gate + closeout gate + toolchain prerequisite gate before runtime-heavy steps.
post_update_steps: Run /init_brain after /update_brain to reload rules, MCP registration, and skill routing.
Open project-root .mcp.json and fill FIGMA_ACCESS_TOKEN only if you want Figma MCP.
Approve new MCP servers in your client the first time they are offered.
Run `python3 .agents/scripts/validate_command_surface.py --root .` after merging/updating `.agents` to confirm command contracts stayed aligned.
Use /doc_reconcile before changing behavior on brownfield projects with missing or stale docs.
If using /refactor-planning, ensure ESLint is available (global or local node_modules/.bin) so the toolchain gate passes before runtime-heavy steps.
onboarding_prompts: Try `/marcus.routecheck` to see how routing regression is enforced after major workflow changes.
Try `/design` and verify gates: `validate_design_readiness.py` and `validate_design_outputs.py` enforce deterministic closeout.
Try `/refactor-planning` and note the new prerequisite chain: readiness -> toolchain -> runtime -> closeout.
Review `SLASH_COMMAND_REGISTRY.md` to see the canonical command-to-script mapping for this V32 surface.
